whatsNew: Marketing surfaces get their missing half. The rulebook allowed a Cinematic tier (DESIGN-LANGUAGE §43) but never said how to build it — now /ss-motion carries a Cinematic appendix (adapted from MengTo/Skills, MIT): GSAP + ScrollTrigger + Lenis tokens (scrub 0.8–1.4, reveals 0.75–1.1s, word/line/card staggers, 'top 82%' trigger), a library ladder from CSS to Three.js, and a mandatory reduced-motion recipe — app/data surfaces stay framer-motion-only. DESIGN-LANGUAGE gains a layered elevation ramp (three shadow tiers with a ring layer — an alternative shadow language, one per project, card alphas snapped to Golden Rule 7's ≤8%), Landing & Pricing composition recipes (Recipes 5/6 — one conversion goal per page, one featured pricing tier), a marketing section vertical rhythm (96–192px on the 6px grid — py-32/128px is named as the violation it is), and WebGL/canvas + media-resolution guardrails (pixel-ratio cap 2, dispose on unmount, offscreen RAF pause, reduced-motion still frame). /ss-restyle grows three presets — dark-glass, paper-warm, editorial-serif. /ss-lint gains a layout-property-animation check; /ss-review gains JS-off content visibility and transform/opacity-only checks.
update: Run /ss-update, or: npx skills add bitjaru/styleseed
